observations.recommendation:
We recommended management to require the Municipal Accountant to record all taxes due to the government using the prescribed account Due to BIR and not transfer year-end balances to Accounts Payable/Due to Other Funds or Due to Officers and Employees in order reflect the correct balances in the financial statements.  Hence, we suggest the following adjustments.

Accounts Payable  	       1,487,562.12
Due to O/E  	                        138,221.84 
Due to Other Funds	          198,688.95
Due to BIR                         1,824,472.91

Further, we also recommended that taxes paid by the GF be immediately refunded by the SEF or TF.

We recommended that management fast-track the synchronization of data between the assessment roll by the Municipal Assessor with the certified list of taxpayers by the Municipal Treasurer to ensure the accuracy of the computed real property taxes collectible – both delinquent taxes and taxes due for the current year.

We recommended that management implement immediately and accordingly the purpose of trust receipts. Otherwise, evaluate the need of these unexpended balances of aid/financial assistance from different source agencies. Should these funds prove to be no longer needed, revert to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

We recommended that the management exert efforts in filling-up the vacant plantilla positions subject to existing civil service laws, rules and regulations. Likewise, we recommended that management properly evaluate the needs of every office or department to augment the manpower requirement to ensure fast and satisfactory delivery of services to the general public.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the disbursement vouchers together with monthly trial balances and journals on the 20th day of the following month as duly required in the NGAS Manual to facilitate the verification of transactions and reports of the LGU.  Ensure that all supporting documents are complete before processing the claims.

We recommended that management has to see to it that allowable funding source for the payment of benefit (CNA) is in accordance with Section 4.3.1. of DBM BC 2021-3.  Further, we recommended the officials and employees to refund immediately the excess payment of the 2020 and 2021 CNA benefits.

We recommended that management stop the practice of procuring office supplies and equipment on staggered basis thru alternative mode of small value procurement and adhere to the provisions of Section 7.2 and 7.3 of RA 9184 which requires that all procurement shall be done through competitive public bidding.  Limit the use of the alternative modes of procurement only when the circumstances warrant which fall within the provisions of Section 52 and Section 53 of RA 9184.  Henceforth, instruct the respective end-users of the different offices to meticulously and religiously plan the procurement of office supplies needed for the efficient operations of the LGU.

We recommended that management submit explanation on the discrepancies noted during the initial review of the contracts.  It is recommended that the excessive contract amount caused by the higher tax rate which had disadvantaged the government be refunded by the persons liable.   Henceforth, it is reminded that the BAC review and recalculate properly all bid proposals to ensure correct computations and compliant with laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that management require the respective barangays to return the unspent financial assistance given to them because it had lapsed the period for implementation so that other recipients can benefit therefrom.  Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant has to impose immediate utilization and liquidation of fund transfers within the reglementary period, otherwise, disqualify non-compliant barangays from receiving financial assistance/fund transfer.

We recommended and the Municipal Accounting Office agreed to trace the entries made in the Government Equity account in the Trust Fund and prepare adjusting journal entry to correct the misstatement from prior years pursuant to the applicable accounting standards.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile the balances of reciprocal accounts and prepare necessary adjusting entries thereof. Any balances which require more time to reconcile like inherited balances shall be disclosed in the notes to the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to immediately stop the accrual of revenues other than the IRA and RPT. Subsequently, she agreed to reconcile with the individual records of the related economic enterprise and determine which accrued revenues remain outstanding. Lastly, outstanding accrued revenues shall be closed as follows.

We recommended and the management agreed to require the Inventory Committee to prioritize and ensure full implementation of the “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006. The management also agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO/Municipal Treasurer to maintain and update the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) and Property Cards (PC) for proper monitoring and reconciliation.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and the Internal Audit Service agreed to monitor and remind the Municipal Mayor’s Office on the timely submission of the Confidential Fund transmittal letters with complete documents to the Audit Team Leader within the period specified in the Joint Circular.

We recommended and the LDRRMO agreed to submit  the Monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF not later than the 15th day after the end of each month to enable the auditor to conduct the timely review, verification and monitoring of the sources and utilization of LDRRMF, thus deficiencies if any, shall be communicated to management for the latter’s immediate and appropriate action.

We recommended that management discontinue the practice of granting cash advances to accountable officers in amounts exceeding their corresponding maximum cash accountability. Otherwise, the management increase the fidelity bond of the AO to cater to the maximum cash accountability per cash advance drawn.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to send demand letters for receivables attributable to private individuals and businesses. Furthermore, the management agreed to avail of the civil remedies if collection is difficult to enforce.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to record transactions to its appropriate accounts as required in COA Circular No. 2015-009 in order to present accurate balances of the accounts in the year-end financial statements.  We also require the adjustment of the payables that remained unrecognized as of year- end to with:

Suggested entries follow:

Depreciation Expense	          127,829.42
Prior Year's Adjusment               12,631.17
Accum.Depreciation                    140,461.13

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to use appropriate account titles in recording prepayments of real property tax and the related discounts, reconcile their records with the Municipal Treasurer’s records, and effect adjusting journal entries to bring the affected accounts to their accurate balances. Henceforth, ensure the regular reconciliation of records between the Accounting Office and the Treasury Office to avoid variances in recorded amounts.

We recommended that trust funds have to be used only for the intended purpose of the fund.  It has to be managed based on careful selection of transactions that are allowable to be charged to avoid disallowances in audit at the same time, cater to the needs of the beneficiaries for which the financial assistance was granted.

We recommended and management agreed to ensure that all procurements undertaken are within the approved annual procurement plan and the HOPE has to approve any changes in the original APP to ensure that only projects that are crucial to the effective discharge of government mandates are included.

We recommended that management issue a Certificate of Final Acceptance one year after the completion date and require a warranty security in accordance with Section 62.2.3.4 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to ensure that the contractor is held liable for any structural defects and failures that might occur on the infrastructure project within the applicable period.

We recommended that management require an explanation from the BAC on the absence of the approval of the HOPE that authorizes the SPP.  Henceforth, all Annual or Supplemental Procurement Plans shall bear the approval of the HOPE or his designated second ranking official.

We recommended that the management, through its MDRRMO, comply with COA Circular No. 2012-02 dated September 12, 2012 and NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013 by ensuring that the LDRRMFIP shall include the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years. Further, submit the investment plan for those expenditures charged to the STF.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and the LDRRMC strictly adhere to the guidelines set under the pertinent sections of JMC No. 2013-1 in the appropriation and utilization of the LDRRMF by not charging the expenses related to the repairs and maintenance of the vehicles.
We recommended that management require the AAO to perform his responsibilities as required by GSIS so that similar incidents will be avoided in the future.
We recommended that management stop granting load allowance to COS since their contracts clearly stipulates that they are not entitled to benefits enjoyed by regular employees.

We recommended and management agreed that brand names of the medicines be indicated on the Purchase Order as reference to the item being bid out and actual delivery. Further, we require compliance of the documentary requirements per COA Circular 2012-001 for subsequent procurement of medicines.

We recommended that management review the projects appropriated in the current and continuing appropriations and determine if they are still feasible. Henceforth, ensure that developmental projects incorporated in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) for the 20% Local Development Fund are implementation ready within an acceptable time frame.

We recommended that management require the MSWDO to coordinate with the punong barangays and other focal persons to ensure that beneficiaries are properly validated to avoid duplication in the listing or inclusion of disqualified beneficiaries.  Henceforth, the MSWDO has to update its records of all the low-income and vulnerable sectors of the society to ensure that they are the actual and valid recipients of any cash assistance extended by the government.

We recommended and management agreed to refrain from passing ordinances for salary differential for JOs and COs because this is contrary to laws, rules and regulations nearing year-end.  To address the financial constraints of these JOs and COs who belongs to the low income group, strategized and evaluate the necessary JOs and COs so that the daily wage stipulated in the contracts are much higher than the existing rates paid to these personnel.

We recommended that management see to it that acquisition of land has to be in accordance with the provisions of RA 10792. Also, submit an explanation of its non-adherence to the requirements on the purchase of lot by government agencies. It is recommended further that any future transactions involving purchase of real property has to be in accordance with the rules and regulations to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended that management require all officials and employees concerned and responsible in the preparation, approval of trip tickets and authorization of passenger/s to ensure that driver’s trip ticket is duly and properly accomplished and approved before undertaking the trip. Also, require the preparation of the Monthly Report of Official Travel for each vehicle and the Fuel Consumption Report, and submit the same to the auditor for evaluation of the reasonableness of fuel consumption.

Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to withhold the payment of the salaries or any amount due to the persons liable for the settlement of their liabilities pursuant to the ND/Decision and COA Order of Execution;

Require the Municipal Accountant to demand settlement/payment of the unpaid balance of disallowance from municipal officials/employees who had already separated from service.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the Government by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support the release of financial aid and annual dues to the latter, pursuant to Section 69 of the GAAM, Volume I.

It is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall furnish the accountant duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year.  Based on the list, the Municipal Accountant shall prepare the journal entry vouchers for the setting up of Real Property Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 – Reiterated in Part II of this report | The amount set-up for CY 2021 was based on the delinquent taxes and not on the taxpayer’s index cards because the tax declarations of sold lots were not yet updated.  If the target collections will be based on the tax payers’s index, there is a possibility of double recording.
observations.reason: The audit team conducted analysis of the collections of real property tax during the year and noted that the amount set-up was indeed lower than the actual collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to that management submit explanation of its non-adherence to the provisions of RA 10752 on the acquisition of lot by government agencies. It is recommended further that any future transactions involving purchase of real property has to be in accordance with the established rules and regulations to prevent irregular disbursement of funds.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | Management paid in full the subsequent acquisitions of lot such as the bus terminal because the owners will not agree to sell the property if not fully paid.  Further, one of the owners is already old and need the proceeds to finance their medical needs. | The price of lot offered to the owner was based on the Provincial Assessor’s assessment and lower than the market value of adjoining lots.  The Municipal Assessor has started to have the lot applied for a special patent at the DENR.
observations.reason: The price of lot offered to the owner was based on the Provincial Assessor’s assessment and lower than the market value of adjoining lots.  The Municipal Assessor has started to have the lot applied for a special patent at the DENR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Finance Committee evaluate and review the budget proposals meticulously before the LCE submits the executive budget to the Local Sanggunian for enactment.  It is necessary that the budget be thoroughly reviewed to avoid incidents of bloated income estimates which can affect sound financial management of the agency.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2019 | The 2021 TCH budget still have high income estimates because of the expenses such as double set of manpower because of the Covid 19 ward.  Due to health protocols, health front liners are not allowed to work in non-Covid ward, thus the need to maintain two groups of manpower resources.  Further, some admissions in TCH come from other municipalities and they cannot refuse their admissions.

They have to track the application in DOH for Level I hospital classification. The application was deferred because of Covid 19 pandemic.
observations.reason: Our review of the individual financial statements of the TCH for CY 2021 P13.8M showed that it has again incurred net loss of ₱13.8M and a negative position of Ᵽ18.9M.  It was discussed during the exit conference that management will try to make representations with DOH for the possibility of turn-over of the hospital under the administration of the DOH.

observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 | Management still has to contact the supplier.
observations.reason: The LGU has to communicate with the supplier.

The Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee has to reconcile the balance of PPE per books with that of the inventory reports, verify and analyze the discrepancies, and prepare adjustments to the affected records.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which should come from the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) of Taxpayer’s Index Card maintained at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and furnish the Municipal Accountant copy of the list to serve as the basis in recording the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable accounts in the books. This is in view of the need to record in the books not mere estimates from the real property taxes but actual receivables from said taxes pursuant to Section 19 and 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1, in order to reflect in the books accurate amounts for RPT and SET Receivables.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to record purchases of supplies and materials to its appropriate inventory account as required in COA Circular No. 2015-009 in order to present an accurate balance of the accounts in the year-end financial statements. Likewise, maintain the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for supplies in order to establish control in the receipt and disposition of the inventory items.

Recommendation:
We recommend management has to see to it that allowable funding source for the payment of benefit (CNA) is in accordance with Section 4.3.1. of DBM BC 2021-3. Further, we recommend the officials and employees to refund immediately the excess payment of the 2020 and 2021 CNA benefits.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management has to see to it that the grant of CNA and other has to be in accordance with the laws, rules and regulations issued by the DBM. Henceforth, see to it that CNA benefits are sourced solely from the allowable MOOE allotments.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management stop the practice of procuring office supplies and equipment on staggered basis thru alternative mode of small value procurement and adhere to the provisions of Section 7.2 and 7.3 of RA 9184 which requires that all procurement shall be done through competitive public bidding. Limit the use of the alternative modes of procurement only when the circumstances warrant which fall within the provisions of Section 52 and Section 53 of RA 9184. Henceforth, instruct the respective end-users of the different offices to meticulously and religiously plan the procurement of office supplies needed for the efficient operations of the LGU.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal officials intensifies its collections and exercise fiscal restraint in the disbursement of funds. Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to limit disbursement of funds to valid obligations only as appropriated in the Approved Annual Budget to avoid the incurrence of any cash overdraft at year end. Furthermore, require the Local Finance Committee to institute remedial and corrective measures to improve its financial standing.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management has to see to it that funding source for the honoraria of the BAC Members is in accordance with DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3. Further, we require that the BAC members refund the payment of the 2021 BAC honoraria that was sourced from the 2020 Surplus due to irregular disbursement.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management evaluate whether some equipment still have to be repaired or purchase new ones rather than maintain those that need constant repairs which is counter-productive. Also, require the periodic assessment and personnel be assigned to oversee the conditions of the motor vehicles and equipment and to provide report on the operational efficiency as basis of management’s action to observe economy and efficiency in the use and operations of the motor vehicles.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management require all concerned officials and employees to exercise due care and diligence for government properties entrusted to them because accountability for any damaged or loss of property is a personal liability if there is no valid cause and there is no evidence that care and diligence for the property are observed. Further, require the end-user to refund the amount paid for the repair of the damaged laptop.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management submit explanation on the discrepancies noted during the initial review of the contracts. It is recommended that the excessive contract amount caused by the higher tax rate which had disadvantaged the government be refunded by the persons liable. Henceforth, it is reminded that the BAC review and recalculate properly all bid proposals to ensure correct computations and compliant with laws, rules and regulations.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management adhere with the rules and regulations of RA 9184 and ensure that bid opportunities be supported by a complete detailed engineering design (DED) so that project implementation will not be hampered.

Recommendation:
We recommend to that management see to it that acquisition of land has to be in accordance with the provisions of RA 10792. Also, submit an explanation of its non-adherence to the requirements on the purchase of lot by government agencies. It is recommended further that any future transactions involving purchase of real property has to be in accordance with the rules and regulations to protect the interest of the government.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management require all officials and employees concerned and responsible in the preparation, approval of trip tickets and authorization of passenger/s to ensure that driver’s trip ticket is duly and properly accomplished and approved before undertaking the trip. Also, require the preparation of the Monthly Report of Official Travel for each vehicle and the Fuel Consumption Report, and submit the same to the auditor for evaluation of the reasonableness of fuel consumption.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management immediately stop the practice of charging to SEF the transactions outside the scope for which this fund was established and observe the proper utilization thereof pursuant to the provisions of Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017 and 2020 of DepEd, DBM and DILG to attain the government's goal in operation and maintenance of elementary and secondary schools. Moreover, require the refund of the improper charge to the SEF.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management observe the guidelines in the payment of the ADHP to avoid conflicts with the duties and responsibilities of the concerned officials and to avoid untoward incidents that may expose them to risk of contracting the Covid-19 virus. Further, require the barangay officials concerned to refund the payment received or cause the deduction of the earned leave credits for CY 2021 equivalent to the days rendered at the quarantine facility.

We recommend management to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to coordinate that charges to the General Fund for payment of taxes and other liabilities be replenished immediately to avoid inaccurate charges and depletion of the general fund.  Further, require the personnel concerned to take extra care in preparation of checks for payment to avoid erroneous charging of transactions.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | The GF is still the fund responsible for all the tax remittances.  However, this time, the refunds to the GF for the taxes in SEF and TF are done simultaneously with the preparation of the tax remittance by the GF.
observations.reason: Checks to refund the GF were already prepared together with the taxes remittance paid by the GF to minimize piling up of unpaid taxes to the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the municipal accountant to keep track of all the types of securities required from the contractors for all its projects undertaken. Further, require Municipal Engineer to observe the proper timeline when projects have to be finally accepted.  It is also recommended for the contractor to issue the required warranty securities for the three (3) projects. Finally, management is enjoined to observe the pertinent guidelines and provisions in the Revised – IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | Management already corrected the past error of issuing in advance Certificate of Final Acceptance and see it that certificate of final acceptance will be released only after the lapse of the Defects and Liability Period.
observations.reason: Most projects are by administration and those contracted are still in the Defects and Liability Period (DLP), thus, will be verified if indeed, the Certificate of Final Acceptance will be released after the lapse of the DLP.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that henceforth management observe BIR rules on the withholding of any forms of taxes due to the government as required by revenue regulations and require the refund of the taxes not withheld from the payment of the creditable and percentage taxes.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | The management thru the Municipal Accountant communicated with the hotel management for the status of the un-withhold taxes in relation to the payment of the hotel accommodation for the OFW.  The hotel management cooperated with the request of the management and provided evidences that taxes were remitted to BIR. Some hotels also refunded to LGU Tubigon such as Belle Pacific and Panglao Bluewater
observations.reason: BIR remittances of the hotels were submitted in support to the implementation of the audit recommendation.  Official Receipts also presented for those hotels that opted to refund the taxes.  Certificate of Creditable Withholding Tax  No. 2307 also issued to the hotels.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management carefully plan public spending of the 70% of the LDRRM so that LGUs can be capacitated to institutionalize measures for reducing disaster risks and hazards and to minimize potential adverse effects when disasters may occur.  Henceforth, realign the charging for the loan amortization to other appropriate fund source.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | The utilization of the 2021 LDRRM did not include the payment of the loan for the heavy equipment.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provide basis for the selection of the supplier if there was cost benefit analysis and if the prices offered are favorable to the government.  Further, we also require explanation from the BAC on resorting to the mode of procurement that circumvented the Procurement Law.  Finally, it is required that management has to abide with the Procurement Law in procurement of goods and services to avoid irregular disbursement of funds.

Further, require the contractors of the abovementioned projects to refund the corresponding costs which should have been excluded in the bids.

observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | Job repairs of air conditioning units undertaken by a Job Order/Contractual Employee to refrain from outsourcing the aircon maintenance.
observations.reason: Outsourcing repairs of aircon minimized.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require those responsible for the procurement to submit written justification for the noted price variance and defying the   DOH Memorandum Nos. 2020-0058 and 2020-0014A that were issued at the height of the pandemic. Henceforth, require the end-users and BAC to abide and be updated with rules and regulations related to Covid 19 procurement for the prevention of abuse and corruption in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 | The requisitions of medicines and drugs supplies already abide by the price lists issued by DOH.  The management justified that refund of the excess payments be reconsidered because at that time, the supply is very scarce and if they will not pursue the supplies available, it can prejudice the health and welfare of the constituents because they cannot provide the needed PPEs which are of outmost priority at the height of the Covid-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: No refunds were made for the overpriced medicines.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management ensure that all disbursement of funds has a valid and legal appropriation, otherwise, management is liable for the unauthorized disbursement.  We require the budget officer submit evidence that there was sufficient budget for the payment of the 2018 PCL Annual Due, otherwise, the excess payment with no budgetary allocation has to be disallowed in audit.  Finally, we require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the entry made by taking up the amount released as Other Receivables.  Finally, adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-003 to avoid disallowances in the disbursement of government funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Budget Officer determine the amount valid to be
recognized as unexpended appropriation and formulate the supplemental budget
to be submitted to the Local Sanggunian for the enactment of an appropriation ordinance.
Further, unexpended PS and MOOE have to be reverted to unappropriated surplus
and any disbursement charged against the continuing appropriation totaling
₱4,929,032.14 has to be disallowed in audit since the utilization thereof was
unauthorized.

We recommend that the Local Finance Committee evaluate and review the
budget proposals meticulously before the LCE submits the executive budget to the Local
Sanggunian for enactment. It is necessary that the budget be thoroughly
reviewed to avoid incidents of bloated income estimates which can affect sound
financial management of the agency.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and the Sanggunian Bayan expedite the
updating and amendment of the Municipality’s Local Revenue Code particularly in
the TCH in pursuance to Section 191 of R.A. 7160 for probable increase in revenue
rates and adoption of additional revenue sources to fund the Municipality’s projects
and programs for the general welfare of its constituents.

We recommend that management require the respective barangays to return the
unspent financial assistance given to them because it had lapse the period for
implementation so that other recipients can benefit therefrom. Henceforth, the
Municipal Accountant has to impose immediate utilization and liquidation of fund
transfers within the reglementary period, otherwise, disqualify non-compliant
barangays from receiving financial assistance/fund transfer.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO)
to check the appropriations properly and allocate for Representation Expenses as these
are regularly incurred in the discharge of the different functions of the department.
Further, the MBO and Municipal Accountant have to coordinate so that the accounts
used by both departments are consistent and will not result in the payment of fund
without the corresponding appropriation.

We recommend that management review and evaluate the need to allocate
additional benefit to the Public Social Workers and if warranted, appropriate
accordingly in the annual budget the benefits due to the social welfare workers.

We recommend that management require the personnel in charge with the
computation of personnel benefits of officials and employees be updated and
knowledgeable of benefits due to officials and employees so that the correct and just
amounts are accorded to them and corresponding PS expenditures are properly
reflected in the books to avoid misstatements thereof. Also, require the MBO to review
the appropriation for each department and ensure that personnel benefits are properly
allocated and the likelihood of budgetary shortfall can be appropriately acted upon.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO)
to determine available funds from savings of the current year’s PS for possible
appropriation of the night shift differential of the public health workers. With the
concurrence of the Sangunian Bayan (SB), taking into consideration of the PS
limitation, supplemental budget may be prepared for the payment of the differential.
Subsequently, the SB has to enact an ordinance for the appropriation of the
night shift differential of health workers in accordance with the R-IRR of RA 7305.

We recommend that management require the GAD Focal Person to ensure the
allocation of at least five percent of the total annual budget of the LGU in compliance
with DBM, NEDA and NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1 dated April 5, 2004 and
Section B, Part 5 of the Updated Budget Operations Manual for Local Government
Units.

We recommend that management require the GAD Focal Person to coordinate
with the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) and look into the deficiencies noted, and
incorporate revisions thereto if warranted. Further, it is recommended that
management maximize the utilization of the GAD fund so that the intended purpose in
addressing gender-related issues can be fully achieved.

We recommend that management with the assistance of the MPDC and other
key players in the decision making of the LGU have to strategize so that projects
identified be implemented within the year to address the needs of the community are
fully attained. Likewise, get the maximum value of fund allocated for a specific period
considering the increasing prices of materials. Further, conduct actual studies for
previously identified projects and determine if those are still feasible and practicable to
be implemented, otherwise, realign funds to other projects most practical and
responsive to the present needs.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to coordinate and act on the return of the remaining funds to
the Bureau of Treasury so that the government can use it for other priority programs
of the government. The following suggested adjusting entries are to be prepared for
the return of the fund:
Due to Other NGAS ₱ 90,618.99
Cash in Bank ₱ 90,618.99

the appropriate adjustment/reversion of the unutilized LDRRM Fund to the General

the AAO has to update immediately the GSIS on all changes of personnel records of the

trace the causes of said unadjusted balances of the aforementioned liability account.

loan and enforce collection of the unpaid loans from its members based on the agreed
terms and conditions, otherwise, the persons responsible for the release of the loan is liable
for the unpaid balances because of the questionable use of the 20% LDF

that mobilization fee be released only when the contractor presents standby letter of
credit, and abide by the implementing rules and regulations of the procurement law.

We recommend that management require the BAC to provide valid and authentic
proofs that the LGU acquired the most advantageous price in procurement of supplies and
materials through alternative methods of procurement. Likewise, the BAC has to ensure
that all procurement activities be in accordance with the prescribed guidelines set forth in
the R-IRR of RA 9184.

We recommend that management require the BAC to provide valid and authentic
proofs that the LGU acquired the most advantageous price in conducting the strategic
planning which we will evaluate. Likewise, the BAC has to ensure that all procurement
activities be in accordance with the prescribed guidelines set forth in the R-IRR of RA
9184.

We recommend that management provide justification for passing an ordinance
which contravened the Procurement Law, which defeats the purpose of competitive
bidding. Further, we recommend that the legislative department propose for the
amendment of Municipal Ordinance No. 2014-398 to be in harmony and consistent with
the Procurement Law.

We recommend that the BAC adhere with the provisions of Municipal Ordinances
particularly on the imposition of cost of bidding documents so that correct fees are being
collected. Further, it is advised that the Municipal Ordinance mentioned be revisited and
amended when necessary.

We recommend that the BAC has to verify that brands and specifications of the
product offered must be indicated on the RFQs process only those requests that indicated
relevant characteristics and specifications. Further, the BAC has to ensure that business
establishments selected to deliver goods wherein prices are most advantageous to the
government. Lastly, we recommend that the BAC strictly adhere to the provisions of the
Procurement Law to protect the interest of the government.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant check the payment of honoraria to
the BAC and BAC Secretariat that has to be in accordance with the guidelines set for in the
Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184. Further, require the BAC
secretariat to refund the excess honorarium received.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer, the executive and legislative
departments of the agency review properly the composition of the budget and ensure that
all items being appropriated in the Annual Budget are in accordance and anchored on
prescribed laws, rules and regulations to avoid disbursements of funds that are not
allowable.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to secure
Official Receipts for all fund transfers to other government agency and the accountant has to
record such as receivables and not as direct debit to expenses account. Further, review
the items billed by the different schools and evaluate if those fees required by the schools
from the learners are authorized by DepEd. Finally, the LGU may pay directly to the
different agencies the authorized contributions such as the GSP, BSP, PNRC, Anti TB, and
the PTA.

We recommend that management ensure that all disbursement of funds has a valid
and legal appropriation; otherwise, management is liable for the unauthorized
disbursement. We require the budget officer submit evidence that there was sufficient
budget for the payment of the 2018 PCL Annual Due, otherwise, the excess payment with
no budgetary allocation has to be disallowed in audit. Finally, we require the Municipal
Accountant to reclassify the entry made by taking up the amount released as Other
Receivables. Finally, adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-003 to avoid
disallowances in the disbursement of government funds.

We recommend that the management provide explanation on the above noted
deficiency and refunds have to be made on the double-payment. Further, scrutinize

regulations. Henceforth, expenses for cultural activities be incurred conscientiously and in

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the designated Deputy Disbursing Officers of the Confidential/Intelligence Fund of the municipality apply for fidelity bond with the Bureau of the Treasury and the Head of Agency evaluate the amount of Fidelity Bond vis–a-vis the corresponding maximum accountability they handled in accordance with the Revised Schedule of Premium Rates of Fidelity Bond.
observations.managementAction: the accountable officer were bonded.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require: a) The Municipal Budget Officer, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records, especially at year end, for the proper reporting of transactions in the municipal books. b) The Municipal Accountant to record in payable accounts, transactions that are incurred during the year but are not paid during that year, in order to reflect the true results of operations for the period.
It is recommended that all municipal officials and regular employees of LGU, Tubigon, Bohol refund the amount of ₱13,500.00 each for the amount they received as CNA Incentive.
observations.managementAction: Refund was made on staggered basis by deducting from the payroll of the salary received by the municipal officials and employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management justify the charging of salaries and wages against MOOE which circumventing the above laws and regulations and which paved the way for the granting of PEI.

We recommend that Management justify/explain why the projects funded out of the 20% development fund were not implemented as planned. We recommended that henceforth, Management ensure prompt and full implementation of the development projects as programmed in order to achieve the intended purpose for which the fund was appropriated and improve the socio-economic and environmental condition of the constituents.
